title: YMCA'S DIABETES PREVENTION PROGRAM - 211info
text: EUGENE FAMILY YMCA YMCA'S DIABETES PREVENTION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The YMCA's Diabetes Prevention Program is a year-long program consisting of 25 classroom sessions. A trained Lifestyle Coach helps participants learn strategies for healthy eating, physical activity, and other lifestyle changes. 600 E 24th Avenue Eugene, OR 97405 Eligibility: Age 18 and older who are overweight with a BMI greater than or equal to 25 and diagnosed with prediabetes or at high risk for diabetes Hours: No standard hours Email (541) 686-9622 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 686-9622 Contact Name: Kate Kevern Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Walk in, call, or visit website for more information FEES: Varies by service DOCUMENTS: None required at intake AREA SERVED: Lane County SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 5:30am-9pm, Saturday-Sunday 7am-7pm Services: Chronic Disease Self Management Programs * Diabetes Other Services Offered At This Location: HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - LIVESTRONG AT THE YMCA LEISURE ACTIVITIES/RECREATION - CAMPS LEISURE ACTIVITIES/RECREATION - U CAN GYM SOCIAL DEVELOPMENT AND ENRICHMENT - COMMUNITY RECREATION CENTER If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. EUGENE FAMILY YMCA
